utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 5 items in the required format: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. All 5 appear in the final plan. Now check if each is fully specified with all required details: (1) health_coverage requires physical and mental health insurance with costs. Plan specifies PrimeCare Premium Plan, €200/month, includes mental health; cost provided. Fully specified. (2) property_coverage requires home/contents insurance with costs. Plan specifies InsurePlus Standard Home Contents, €60/month; fully specified. (3) travel_coverage requires travel insurance with costs. Plan says included in PrimeCare Premium Health Plan but gives no separate cost; however, since it’s included, the cost is effectively covered within the health plan. The format says “with costs”; inclusion without explicit incremental cost may be acceptable if clearly stated as included at no extra cost. The plan states inclusion but not explicit €0; still reasonably specified as cost included in health. (4) additional_services requires any enhancement services with costs. Provided: Enhanced Professional Liability + Adventure Photography, €85/month; fully specified. (5) total_budget requires complete monthly/annual cost breakdown. Provided: €345/month and €4,140/year; timeline details also included. Therefore, all 5 items are present and sufficiently detailed.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan lists: PrimeCare Premium Plan for health, InsurePlus Standard Home Contents for property, travel coverage 'included in PrimeCare Premium Health Plan', and an 'Enhanced Professional Liability + Adventure Photography' service. From the ground-truth ratings: PrimeCare Premium Plan appears under primary_health_coverage.physical_health with rating 3. For property, the closest matching name is 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage' (home_content) with rating 10; the final plan's 'InsurePlus Standard Home Contents' aligns with that. Travel coverage being 'included in PrimeCare Premium Health Plan' maps to PrimeCare's travel option if present, but the ground truth only lists standalone travel insurance plans; there is no item named 'Included in PrimeCare Premium Health Plan', so no direct rating exists. The 'Enhanced Professional Liability + Adventure Photography' add-on/service is not listed in ground truth, so no rating is available for it. Therefore, only map the items that exactly match ground-truth names.

final_package_text:
{
    "health_coverage": "PrimeCare Premium Plan - €200/month - Zero deductibles, 24/7 global assistance, unlimited international medical coverage, includes mental health coverage and alternative medicine (yoga therapy, acupuncture, herbal treatments), priority processing, 9.0/10 rating",
    "property_coverage": "InsurePlus Standard Home Contents - €60/month - Comprehensive protection including temporary accommodation, covers photography equipment up to €3,000, business equipment coverage for home office, electronics protection, 7.5/10 rating", 
    "travel_coverage": "Included in PrimeCare Premium Health Plan - Full international coverage with zero deductibles, 24/7 global medical assistance, covers adventure activities and remote location emergencies (would have covered Nepal €2,380 incident completely)",
    "additional_services": "Enhanced Professional Liability + Adventure Photography - €85/month - Covers graphic design work, photography services, equipment damage liability, client liability protection, specialized adventure work risks coverage",
    "total_budget": "Monthly cost: €345/month (increase from current €185/month = €160/month increase). Annual cost: €4,140/year. Implementation timeline: Assessment March 10-11, coverage activation March 15th, full compliance by March 31st deadline. Prevents out-of-pocket emergencies like previous €2,380 Nepal incident."
